project.name: Champions League Predictor
project.purpose: Interactive dashboard for Champions League Monte Carlo simulation results, with simulation controls, KPI summaries, visual diagnostics, and data export.

frontend_overview.ui_paradigm: Single-page data app with sectioned layout
key_jobs_to_be_done: Understand title probabilities and stage progression
Run new simulation iterations on demand
frontend_overview.experience_goal: Niche clean design with editorial hierarchy, strong readability, and low visual noise.

technology_stack.rendering_mode: Server-side Python UI generation with HTML/CSS overrides via st.markdown

global_order: Data explorer table and CSV export
information_architecture.navigation_style: In-page scrolling with tabbed visualization area

usage.primary_font: Body copy, section text, KPI values

shape_and_depth.shadow_style: Soft layered shadows for cards and hero

visual_design_system.background_system: Dual radial gradients plus neutral base to avoid flat canvas

motion.intent: Subtle staged reveal without distracting micro-interactions

hero.purpose: Set context and communicate product positioning quickly

controls: Primary action button: Run New Simulation

visual_diagnostics_tabs.content_type: Static images generated by matplotlib/seaborn pipeline

sidebar.content: Step-by-step guide for running simulations and reading outputs
sidebar.contrast_fix: Explicit dark text styling on all sidebar descendants

footer.content: Project name, dashboard version, workspace descriptor

results_json_expected_shape.notes: Frontend includes robust fallback parsing for quarterfinal naming variants such as P_Quarterfinal and quarterfinalist.

frontend_logic_flow: Compute KPI values and render cards
On run: execute simulation script and regenerate plots
Display plot tabs from image files
Render probability table and download action

responsiveness_and_layout.container: Max-width constrained block container for readability
mobile_behavior: Reduced hero and card padding under 768px
Natural Streamlit column stacking on narrow widths
desktop_behavior: Wide layout with balanced card grid and tabbed content

accessibility_and_readability.contrast: Dark text tokens over light surfaces; explicit sidebar text override
accessibility_and_readability.hierarchy: Mono utility labels + larger semantic headings
accessibility_and_readability.interaction_clarity: Primary button styling and helper note in simulation panel
known_limits: Static plot images are not screen-reader rich compared to native chart components
Emoji-based labels may vary by OS font support

performance_characteristics.ui_impact: Spinner and status messages mask long-running operations
optimization_notes: Consider caching parsed JSON and computed table for repeated renders
Consider async/background simulation queue for large iteration counts

implemented: Subprocess return code check for simulation
error_handling_in_frontend.user_messages: Uses warning, info, success, and error Streamlit statuses

why_this_style: Avoids generic blue-purple AI dashboard look
Uses restrained palette and editorial typography for credibility
Maintains analytical utility while improving emotional polish
ux_tradeoffs: Static images are stable and simple but less interactive
Single-page flow is fast but can become long on smaller screens

future_frontend_improvements: Replace static PNG plots with native interactive chart components
Add theme toggle between presentation and analyst density modes
Introduce team crest visual encoding in table and KPI cards
Add confidence interval overlays for probability uncertainty
Add keyboard-accessible quick-jump links to major sections

integration_notes.dependency_note: Frontend relies on generated JSON and plot assets; if missing, app degrades gracefully with warnings.
